{"id":"ibudget-8lc.15","title":"Database migration strategy for production","description":"Ensure Supabase migrations can be applied cleanly to a fresh production database. Document migration workflow. Consider using Supabase CLI for migration management. No dev seed data should run in production.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"charles.scholle1@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-08T03:22:12Z","created_by":"chaz8080","updated_at":"2026-03-08T03:22:12Z"}

new_value:
{"notes":"# Database Migration Strategy — Production Beta

## Current State
- 8 migrations (00001-00008) define the full schema
- Migrations 00001-00007: core tables, RLS, triggers, PowerSync publication, recurring_transactions
- Migration 00008: RLS policy fixes (owner self-add, leave household, view recurring txns)
- SCHEMA DRIFT FOUND: PowerSync client schema (src/db/schema.ts) has 5 columns on recurring_transactions not in any Supabase migration: by_day_of_week, by_month_day, by_set_pos, end_type, end_count. These work locally (PowerSync creates columns in SQLite) but will fail to sync since Supabase has no matching columns. A new migration (00009) must be created to add these columns before launch.

## 1. Applying Pending Migration (00008) + Schema Drift Fix

### Pre-flight
- Log into Supabase Dashboard > SQL Editor, run: SELECT schemaname, policyname FROM pg_policies WHERE tablename IN ('household_members','recurring_transactions');
- Confirm the 3 policies from 00008 do NOT already exist (avoid duplicate policy errors)

### Apply 00008
Option A (preferred): supabase db push --linked (from project root, with Supabase CLI linked to prod project)
Option B (manual): Copy contents of 00008_fix_rls_policies.sql into Dashboard SQL Editor and run

### Create & Apply 00009 (schema drift fix)
Create supabase/migrations/00009_add_recurring_recurrence_fields.sql:
  ALTER TABLE public.recurring_transactions
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS by_day_of_week TEXT,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS by_month_day TEXT,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS by_set_pos INTEGER,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS end_type TEXT DEFAULT 'never',
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS end_count INTEGER;
Apply same way as 00008.

### Verify
- Run: SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'recurring_transactions' ORDER BY ordinal_position;
- Run: SELECT policyname FROM pg_policies WHERE tablename = 'household_members';
- Confirm recurring_transactions has all 5 new columns
- Confirm household_members has "Owner can add self to household" and "Users can remove own membership" policies

## 2. Future Migration Workflow

### Local Development
1. Write migration SQL in supabase/migrations/NNNNN_description.sql
2. Test locally: supabase db reset (applies all migrations + seed)
3. If PowerSync schema changes, update src/db/schema.ts to match
4. If sync scope changes, update powersync/sync-rules.yaml
5. Run npm run lint to verify TypeScript still compiles

### Deploying to Production
1. supabase db push --linked (applies only new/unapplied migrations)
2. If sync-rules.yaml changed, update in PowerSync dashboard (or deploy via powersync CLI)
3. Verify: spot-check a few queries in Supabase SQL Editor
4. Test the app against production

### Naming Convention
NNNNN_verb_noun.sql (e.g., 00010_add_tags_to_transactions.sql)
Keep migrations small and focused. One concern per file.

## 3. Backup Strategy

### Before Each Migration
- Supabase Dashboard > Database > Backups > create a manual backup (Pro plan, daily auto-backups already run)
- For free tier: pg_dump via supabase db dump > backup_YYYY-MM-DD.sql (store locally, do NOT commit)
- For the beta (small dataset), a dump takes seconds

### Point-in-Time Recovery
- Available on Pro plan (7-day PITR window)
- For family beta on free tier: manual pg_dump before each migration is sufficient

## 4. PowerSync Sync Rules & Schema Changes

### How They Relate
- Supabase migrations change the PostgreSQL schema (source of truth)
- src/db/schema.ts defines what PowerSync stores in local SQLite (must match columns you query)
- powersync/sync-rules.yaml defines WHAT data syncs to each user (row-level filtering)
- The "powersync" publication (created in 00006) defines WHICH tables are replicated

### When to Update Each
| Change | Migration | schema.ts | sync-rules.yaml | Publication |
|--------|-----------|-----------|------------------|-------------|
| New table | Yes | Yes | Yes (add query) | ALTER PUBLICATION powersync ADD TABLE |
| New column on existing table | Yes | Yes (if client needs it) | No (SELECT * catches it) | No |
| New RLS policy | Yes | No | No | No |
| Change sync filtering | No | No | Yes | No |

### Deployment Order for Schema Changes
1. Apply Supabase migration first (add columns/tables)
2. Update PowerSync publication if new table
3. Deploy updated sync-rules.yaml to PowerSync dashboard
4. Deploy app update with new schema.ts
(Order matters: backend schema must exist before clients try to sync new columns)

## 5. Rollback Approach

### For Additive Changes (ADD COLUMN, CREATE TABLE, new policies)
- Generally safe and backward-compatible — old app versions ignore new columns
- If rollback needed: write a new forward migration that drops/reverts (never edit applied migration files)
- Example: 00010_revert_00009.sql with DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS

### For Destructive Changes (DROP COLUMN, ALTER TYPE, remove policies)
- NEVER apply destructive changes directly in production
- Use a 2-phase approach:
  Phase 1: Deploy app that stops using the column, keep column in DB
  Phase 2: After all clients updated, drop column in next migration

### If a Migration Fails Mid-Way
- Supabase wraps each migration in a transaction — partial applies are auto-rolled back
- Fix the SQL, re-run supabase db push
- If something slips through: restore from backup (pg_dump taken pre-migration)

### Emergency: Full Restore
- Restore from manual backup: psql < backup_YYYY-MM-DD.sql
- Or use Supabase PITR (Pro plan)
- PowerSync clients will re-sync automatically after DB is restored

## Seed Data
- supabase/seed.sql inserts a test user (test@ibudget.local) — this runs ONLY on supabase db reset (local dev)
